title: Keep Your ID and Documents Safe
summary: Store your identification documents securely and know what to do if they are lost.
learnerPromise: You will be able to store your important IDs and documents safely and know the first steps to take if they go missing.
whyItMatters: Your ID and documents are key to daily life — from travel and banking to proving who you are. Keeping them safe and knowing what to do if they are lost protects you from stress, delays, and possible identity problems.
realLifeUses: You need to keep your school ID, driver's license, or passport in a safe place
You want to protect your Social Security card or birth certificate from theft or damage
You lost your wallet and need to know what to do first
You are traveling and need to keep documents secure while away from home
materialsNeeded: A safe storage spot (wallet, folder, or small lockbox)
A list of your important documents
Optional: a phone or camera to take photos of documents
result: Your important documents are stored in a secure place and you have a simple plan for what to do if they are lost.
youWillLearn: Which documents are most important to protect
How to choose a safe place to store ID at home and when going out
How to make a quick list or photo record of your documents
What steps to take first if an ID or document goes missing
miniChallenge: Gather your most important IDs and documents, put them in one secure place, and write a list of what you have. Take photos of the front and back of each card.
steps: Gather your important documents: ID card, health insurance card, and any other key cards
Choose one secure place at home to keep documents you do not carry daily
Decide what you really need to carry in your wallet and leave the rest at home
Write a list of your documents and any important numbers from them
Store the list separately from the documents and consider taking photos as a backup
completionCriteria: Your important documents are stored in a chosen secure location
You have a written list or photo record stored separately from the originals
commonProblems: You carry too many cards and might lose them — only carry what you need that day
You cannot remember what was in your lost wallet — this is why a list helps
You are not sure what to do if something goes missing — start by calling the issuing office
tips: Never carry your Social Security card in your wallet unless absolutely necessary
Keep documents in a dry place away from direct sun to prevent damage
If you lose an ID, report it to the issuing office as soon as you can
